title: 从人群心理健康视角出发的高密度城市地区城市更新研究
titleEn: Urban renewal in high-density urban areas from the perspective of crowd psychological health
summary: 快速城市化显著提高了城市密度，也放大了心理健康压力等相关问题。与此同时，传统建筑存量逐渐饱和，使城市更新的重要性进一步凸显。现有研究常从单一视角切入，例如使用 3D-Isovist 分析行人视觉关系、通过传感器数据量化心理感知，或聚焦城市绿化配置优化。尽管这些方法具有价值，但往往将影响因素割裂处理，因此有必要建立整合多维要素的整体性框架。
summaryEn: Accelerated urbanization has led to significantly increased urban density, amplifying associated challenges including strains on mental well-being. Concurrently, the saturation of traditional building stock emphasizes the critical importance of urban renewal initiatives. Current research in this domain often adopts singular perspectives: examples include analyzing pedestrian visual relationships using 3D-Isovist techniques, quantifying psychological perceptions through sensor-based data collection, or focusing on optimizing urban greenery configurations. While valuable, these approaches typically examine factors in isolation. A holistic framework integrating multiple dimensions is essential.

title: 基于双重机器学习框架的废弃工矿区光伏潜力评估：以孝义市为例
titleEn: Assessment of Photovoltaic Potential in Abandoned Industrial Mining Areas Based on a Double Machine Learning Framework: A Case Study of Xiaoyi City
summary: 在中国式现代化能源转型背景下，废弃工矿区具备光伏部署潜力，但仍需要可靠评估太阳能资源与装机容量。本研究构建面向大型废弃矿区的预测框架，评估地面与屋顶光伏潜力。以山西省孝义市为例，研究通过 Grasshopper 与 ArcGIS Pro 建立三维建模和地块形态聚类流程，形成四类地块共 54 个样本，并使用 Ladybug Tools 模拟建筑能耗强度与太阳辐射。
summaryEn: Within the ongoing energy transition of Chinese-style modernization, abandoned industrial mining areas are promising sites for photovoltaic deployment (PV), but require reliable assessment of solar resources and capacity. This study develops a prediction framework for large abandoned mining sites, evaluating ground and rooftop PV potential. Using Xiaoyi City, Shanxi Province, China, we mapped regional PV potential. A three-dimensional modeling and parcel-morphology clustering workflow using Grasshopper and ArcGIS Pro produced 54 samples across four parcel types. Building energy use intensity (EUI) and solar radiation were simulated with Ladybug Tools.
